description: Weighing just 681 grams when fully packed, the Alpine Pad 25 180 perfectly encapsulates our ‘light and fast’ philosophy. The inflatable, mummy-style design (which Montbell developed, by the way!) strikes the perfect balance between weight, compactness and comfort. Foam padding makes proceedings mighty comfortable, and the 30-denier ripstop outer shell gives you that little extra peace-of-mind when camping upon rough terrain.

description: Our Big Tarp HX is an excellent lightweight shelter, ideal for gram-conscious thru-hikers and long-distance bikepackers. It’s made from a flame-resistant, 75-denier polyester taffeta that’s been coated with waterproof polyurethane, with up to 1,500 millimetres of water protection. There are six lashing holes, allowing you to pitch the tarp as you please and is compatible with our Aluminium Tarp Poles. Reflective guylines, aluminium pegs and a stuff sack all come included.

description: Intended for laid-back camping trips, our Chronos Dome 2 is an expansive and accommodating two person tent. We’ve maximised living space with our patented Vertical Cross system. The poles cross and point outward at 90 degrees at the apex, vastly increasing overhead space. This, coupled with the spacious vestibule to store mucky gear and the like, gives you plenty of space to rest and chill. The rainfly comes equipped with two mesh panels for excellent breathability, and the seam sealed corners are extremely effective against foul weather.

description: The Down Hugger 650 #3 is one of our most versatile sleeping bags. Designed for four season use, the sleeping bag has a comfort temperature rating of -1°C, but can go as low as -7°C when bolstered correctly. This isn't just down to the deployment of premium 650-fill power down (although that certainly doesn’t hurt!) - a roster of innovative design features contribute heavily as well. There’s many small, elastic gathered threads within the quilting and the seams have been strategically oriented. These two features enable the sleeping bag to move naturally and draw insulation closer to your body as you change positions throughout the night, eliminating drafty dead spaces and keeping body heat where it belongs. But that’s not all. Our patented Super Spiral Stretch System reliably retains heat, the two way zip is insulated to shut out cold air, and has been reinforced with triangulated gussets for extra durability.

description: One for the deep winter campers, our Exceloft Air Pad 180 is an extremely warming and lightweight sleeping mat. It’s insulated with Exceloft, a synthetic fibre that we’ve chosen for its warmth-to-packability. The Pad will bolster the comfort rating of your sleeping system, meaning you can still get out for a night under the stairs when the temperature is less favourable. The outer shell is made from hardy 30-denier ripstop as well, giving you that extra peace-of-mind when camping upon rough terrain.

description: Spacious enough to accommodate the family, yet lightweight enough for long-distance bikepacking trips, the Rera Dome 4 is a versatile three season tent. The canopy is a highly breathable mesh fabric that vents unwanted heat. The rainfly and floor are weather-resistant taffeta, with up to 1,500 and 2,000 millimetres of water protection, respectively. Throw in seam-sealed corners and you’ve got a completely comprehensive shelter from the elements. The spacious front vestibule easily accommodates a lot of mucky gear, and expansive overhead pockets help keep essentials organised. The tent comes with reflective guylines as well.

Throw in seam-sealed corners and you’ve got a co

description: Our Seamless Alpine Burrow Bag #2 is a fantastic option if you’re looking to camp in wet or humid conditions. This is because it utilises synthetic insulation which, unlike down, stays warm even when it’s wet. Using our specialised EXCELOFT insulation, the bag is temperature rated to 1°C, but can go as low as -5°C when bolstered correctly. The double-layer construction is reliably warm, and features our patented Super Spiral Stretch system on the interior. This retains heat by moving naturally with your body when you’re tossing-and-turning in the night and draws insulation closer to you. Which is exactly where you want it to be!

description: If you’re camping in cold and wet conditions, you could do worse - a lot worse, in fact - than our Seamless Burrow Bag #2. The bag is made with our proprietary Exceloft, a synthetic insulation that, unlike down, performs even when wet. The heat-retaining properties are enhanced by our seamless construction, which traps the warm air that’d usually escape through sewing holes and reduces cold spots. And then there’s the litany of thoughtful details, all unified in the aim of keeping you as warm as possible. The tunnel hood limits head exposure, the Draft Tube zipper is insulated and the adjustable neck baffle locks warmth where it belongs. All of these factors combined gives the Burrow Bag a comfort rating of 0°C, but can go down to as low as -6°C when bolstered correctly.

description: Our Seamless Down Hugger 800 #3 is a state-of-the-art sleeping bag. Its light weight (clocking in at a minimal 531g) doesn’t come at the expense of performance, as it has a comfort temperature rating of 4°C. It can even go as low as -1°C when bolstered correctly. How’ve we achieved this? Well-thought-out details and premium materials of course! Insulated by 800-fill power EX down, our most premium down, the exterior of the sleeping bag is made with our innovative baffle-less Spider System, specifically designed to not restrict loft. On the interior is our patented Super Spiral Stretch System, a feature that reliably retains heat by moving naturally with your body when you’re tossing-and-turning in the night and draws insulation closer to you. Finally, an insulated zip guard shuts out cold air, and is reinforced with triangulated gussets for extra durability.

description: Our Seamless Down Hugger 800 #7 Long is the lightest in our Down Hugger range, clocking in at a mere 500 grams. Insulated by 800-fill power EX down, our most premium down, the exterior of the sleeping bag is made with our innovative baffle-less Spider System, specifically designed to not restrict loft. On the interior is our patented Super Spiral Stretch System, a feature that reliably retains heat by moving naturally with your body when you’re tossing-and-turning in the night and draws insulation closer to you. Finally, an insulated zip guard shuts out cold air, and is reinforced with triangulated gussets for extra durability. Designed for fair weather camping, it has a comfort temperature rating of 11°C, but can go as low as 7°C when bolstered correctly.

description: One of our lightest sleeping mats - clocking in at 514 grams when packed - the U.L. Air Pad 180 is a fantastic all-rounder. Thanks to its flat, stable surface, it’s ideal for side and restless sleepers. Then there’s the hardy 30-denier ripstop outer shell, which gives you that extra peace-of-mind when camping upon rough terrain.

description: Our U.L. Dome Shelter 2 is one of the lightest and most compact tents on the market. A bold claim of course, but we’re confident in our claim. It’s all thanks to the 10-denier Ballistic Airlight nylon ripstop (a fabric we frequently use for our lightest jackets) and a freestanding, single-wall, domed construction. Despite the tent being so thin, it has excellent weather-protection, thanks to the sealed seams that effectively prevent water ingress. The upper part of the entrance is a double-layered structure that allows for ventilation that blocks out water as well. And if all that wasn’t enough, it’s also incredibly easy to pitch thanks to the sleeved poles.

description: The U.L. Mono Frame Shelter is far-and-away our lightest and most compact tent. In fact, it might just be the lightest and most compact the market has to offer. That’s a bold claim, but we’re confident in it. It’s all thanks to the 10-denier Ballistic Airlight nylon ripstop (a fabric we frequently use for our lightest jackets) and the single-wall, single pole construction. The angle of said pole is aligned at a 35 degree angle which vastly increases the surface area of liveable space. Weather protection isn’t an issue either, as the sealed seams effectively prevent water ingress. And for the moisture that builds up on the inside of the tent, there are three strategically-placed ventilation panels for excellent airflow.
